title: Wayfinding Floorplan Configuration
description: Schema for configuring the Revel Digital wayfinding application with location data, floor plan images, and waypoint navigation graphs.

locations.description: List of navigable destinations within the building.

floors.description: Floor definitions including image references and waypoint navigation graphs.

Location.description: A navigable destination such as a room, office, or amenity.

category.description: Category used for grouping and styling.

floor.description: Floor identifier. Must match a floor entry in the 'floors' array.

description.description: Brief description of the location, displayed to visitors.

icon.description: Emoji or icon character displayed alongside the location name.

waypointId.description: ID of the waypoint on the floor's navigation graph that corresponds to this location. Must match a waypoint ID in the relevant floor's waypoints array.

Floor.description: Configuration for a single floor, including its image and navigation waypoints.

floor.description: Floor identifier. Referenced by locations via their 'floor' field.

image.description: URL to the floor plan SVG or image file.

imageSize.description: Image dimensions as [width, height] in pixels. Used to scale the 3D floor plane.

youAreHere.description: Waypoint ID for the visitor's starting position on this floor (e.g. building entrance or elevator).

waypoints.description: Navigation graph nodes. Waypoints are connected to form walkable paths for route calculation.

Waypoint.description: A node in the floor's navigation graph. Waypoints represent key positions (room entrances, corridor intersections, elevator landings) and their connections define walkable paths.

id.description: Unique waypoint identifier. Referenced by location 'waypointId' and other waypoints' 'connections'.

x.description: X position in image-pixel coordinates (0 = left edge).

y.description: Y position in image-pixel coordinates (0 = top edge).

connections.description: IDs of directly connected waypoints. Connections are bidirectional — if A lists B, B should also list A.

isTransport.description: If true, this waypoint represents a cross-floor transport (elevator or stairwell). Its connections may include waypoint IDs on other floors.

transportName.description: Display label for transport waypoints (e.g. 'Main Elevator', 'East Stairwell').
